rationale:
Intuition: recipe_cook.html uses a two-tab toggle (Ingredients / Directions) so the ingredients list is completely hidden while you're stepping through instructions. The swipeable step-card layout Casey likes is solid, but having to tap back to the Ingredients tab mid-cook to check a quantity breaks flow.

What I'd fix: the structural problem is a hard either/or tab toggle (body.view-ingredients class) instead of a persistent split layout. Ingredients should always be visible alongside the steps so you never lose your place.

How: All changes in middleware/templates/recipe_cook.html. Restructure the cook-stage into a CSS flex container with two children: a persistent .ingredients-rail (left, ~260px, scrollable, always visible) and a .steps-pane (right, keeps the existing scroll-snap step cards). Remove the tab-toggle buttons and the body.view-ingredients CSS class. On narrow viewports (phone portrait, <640px) collapse the rail into a collapsible accordion above the first step so the mobile experience stays usable. Add a lightweight JS pass that, when a step card comes into view (IntersectionObserver or the existing swipe handler), bolds the ingredient names that appear in that step's text and dims the rest — so the rail stays scannable without reading the whole list each time. Keep all existing step-card animation, timer, and font-size logic untouched.
instructions:
File to edit: /opt/benson/middleware/templates/recipe_cook.html

1. REMOVE the tab-toggle UI:
   - Delete the two tab buttons (Ingredients / Directions) from the header/nav area.
   - Remove any JS that toggles `body.view-ingredients` or equivalent class.
   - Remove CSS rules for `.view-ingredients` and `.tab-btn` (or equivalent selectors used only for the toggle).

2. RESTRUCTURE the cook-stage into a two-pane flex layout:
   - Wrap the existing stage content in a `<div class="cook-layout">` with `display: flex; flex-direction: row; height: 100%; overflow: hidden;`.
   - Left child: `<div class="ingredients-rail">` — pull the existing ingredients list markup (ingredient name + quantity/unit rows) into this div. Style it: width ~260px, flex-shrink: 0, overflow-y: auto, padding, subtle background differentiation (e.g. slightly darker or lighter than the steps pane).
   - Right child: `<div class="steps-pane">` — contains the existing swipeable step cards exactly as-is (scroll-snap, animations, step counter, timer button all unchanged).

3. RESPONSIVE: add a `@media (max-width: 640px)` rule:
   - `.cook-layout` switches to `flex-direction: column`.
   - `.ingredients-rail` becomes a collapsible `<details><summary>Ingredients</summary>…</details>` (or use a CSS height-transition toggle via a `.rail-open` class on the layout). Height when collapsed: show only the summary row (~44px). Height when expanded: ~40vh, overflow-y: auto. Default: collapsed on small screens.
   - `.steps-pane` takes remaining height/space.

4. ADD ingredient-highlighting JS:
   - After the existing step-navigation JS (keep all existing logic intact), add a new function `highlightIngredientsForStep(stepIndex)`.
   - It reads the text content of the current step card.
   - It iterates over all `.ingredient-row` elements in the rail; for each, checks if the ingredient name (data attribute or the name span's text, lowercased, trimmed) appears as a substring in the current step text (lowercased). 
   - Matching rows: add class `.ing-active` (font-weight: bold, full opacity).
   - Non-matching rows: add class `.ing-dim` (opacity: 0.45).
   - If the current step text has no matches at all (e.g. intro/mise-en-place step), clear both classes so everything shows at full opacity (don't leave the whole list dimmed on those steps).
   - Call `highlightIngredientsForStep` whenever the active step changes (hook into the existing swipe/next/prev handlers — wherever `currentStep` is updated, add the call).

5. DO NOT touch: step-card scroll-snap behavior, timer logic, font-size controls, the recipe header (title/yield/time), back-navigation, or any server-side route/template variable (`{{ recipe }}`, `{{ steps }}`, `{{ ingredients }}` etc.).
